summary:
Iteration 4 — Netlify deploy-config bug fix validated. Verified /app/netlify.toml (repo root, base=frontend, publish=frontend/build, [[redirects]] /* -> /index.html 200), /app/frontend/public/_redirects, and the produced /app/frontend/build/. Confirmed build/index.html contains
, Typekit stylesheet (use.typekit.net/kiu8ndx.css), Cormorant Garamond, and the favicon URL. Confirmed build/_redirects has '/* /index.html 200'. Served the build locally with `npx serve -s build -l 5555` (which emulates Netlify's SPA fallback) and hit /, /work, /work/manufacturing-performance, /about, /writing, /writing/the-clarity-problem, /writing/inside-out, /connect, and an unknown deep path — all returned HTTP 200 with the SPA index.html payload. Playwright verified data-testid='work-detail-manufacturing-performance' and data-testid='writing-detail-inside-out' render on their deep-link URLs with 0 console errors. Regression on preview URL still passes: home hero, 2 work cards, 8 writing posts, and both deep links render correctly with 0 console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: netlify.toml is well-formed: monorepo base=frontend, publish=frontend/build, yarn install --frozen-lockfile && yarn build; SPA [[redirects]] rule is correct; NODE_VERSION=20 pinned; CI=false to bypass CRA ESLint-warnings-as-errors; NPM_FLAGS=--legacy-peer-deps for React 19; long-cache header for /static/*.

Redundant _redirects file present in both /app/frontend/public/ (source) and /app/frontend/build/ (copied by CRA during build). Content is exactly '/*    /index.html   200'. Both mechanisms present = belt-and-suspenders, safe.

No changes needed. Config is production-ready. When user pushes to Netlify, the site will build from frontend/, publish frontend/build/, and serve index.html for all unknown routes so React Router client-side routing (BrowserRouter) works on refresh/direct load.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This iteration validated Netlify deploy-config only. Static build + SPA fallback confirmed working via local `npx serve -s build`. All 9 URLs return 200 with index.html; Playwright confirms deep-link routes render correct data-testid components; preview URL regression clean (2 work cards, 8 writing posts, 0 console errors). No code changes were made. When Netlify next builds from GitHub, it will read /app/netlify.toml, run yarn build inside /frontend, publish frontend/build, and honor the [[redirects]] rule for SPA fallback. Nothing further to test until user pushes and provides a real *.netlify.app URL.
